Takeaways: One key takeaway was the emphasis on identifying and focusing on essential concepts that truly support long-term learning, especially given the limited instructional time many of us are facing post-pandemic. The idea of categorising content into core, supporting and peripheral learning was quite practical as it helps in streamlining lesson plans and focusing on what’s most meaningful for students. I also appreciated the stress on ensuring conceptual clarity rather than simply covering the syllabus.
Suggestions: It would be helpful if future sessions included more specific examples from English, particularly for the secondary level. For instance, how essential concepts apply to areas like grammar, comprehension or literature would make it easier to implement these ideas in real classroom settings. Some sample lesson ideas or assessment tools aligned with essential concepts would also be beneficial.

It highlighted the need to assess not just academic knowledge but also skills like thinking, creativity, and communication. Overall, the session stressed fair, inclusive, and skill-based assessment to support every student’s growth.
